The founder has ADD. His youngest has diagnosed ADHD. His older son has ADD too, undiagnosed but no doubt about it. This came from inside the chaos.

The feeling

You already know exactly what you need to do.
You understand the consequences of not doing it.
You might even know how.
And still. You cannot start.

There's a name for this. Aware paralysis. The gap between knowing and doing, with nothing to explain why it's there. The longer it goes, the worse it gets. And you can't explain it to people who haven't felt it. "Just do it" is what you get. "You were fine yesterday." And you stand there knowing they're right on every practical level and completely wrong on every real level, with no language for the gap.

GoMaiT doesn't ask you to explain yourself.
It just says: right, you're here now.
Let's see what we can do with the next ten minutes.

Months of productive life, not days. Not lost to laziness. Not lost to poor character. Lost to a brain that works differently and has never had the right kind of help.

This is GoMaiT. It doesn't ask you to explain yourself. It just helps you move.

Free to join. No spam. Unsubscribe any time.

See it in action

What it actually looks like

Built in public. Every screen shown is exactly what's being built.

How it works

Three steps. Sixty seconds.
Then Buddy's got you.

No lengthy setup, no streak pressure, no getting organised before it helps you.

1

Name Your Buddy

First thing you do. You give Buddy a name. That's when it becomes yours, not just another app. From then on, Buddy uses it every time.

2

Tell It What's Going On

Voice or text. Add to your Stressometer, hit Cavalry, dump a task into the Elephant Eater. One tap to wherever you need to be.

3

Let Buddy Help You Move

One small step. Not a plan or a system. Just the next thing. Then Buddy steps back and leaves you to it.

The Cavalry Button

When everything feels impossible, there's one button.

One tap from anywhere in the app. No forms. No setup. Buddy reads the room first. Are you okay? Do you need five minutes? Does this need the mental health layer? Then it helps you take the first step. Or just breathes with you.

No other app has this. Not a productivity timer. Not a reminder. The emergency button for when the pile wins. And it wins for all of us sometimes.

Right. Let's sort this.

The Cavalry button

Four options on entry. No judgment on which you choose. Sort the pile. Talk it through. Just breathe. Or get real help.

Meet Buddy

Your AI companion.
Powered by Claude. Named by you.

The first thing you do is give Buddy a name. Then you talk to it, type or voice, whenever you need to. Buddy responds in your chosen mode and uses your name every time. Three modes: Buddy (warm, always), Drill Sergeant (firm, when you need a push), Nanny (gentle, on the hard days). On the Agentic tier, it doesn't just talk. It handles things for you.

Why Claude makes the difference

Most apps use basic AI. GoMaiT uses Claude, built by Anthropic, one of the world's leading AI safety companies, and widely regarded as the most capable conversational AI available. This isn't a chatbot that pattern-matches keywords. Claude understands context, nuance, emotion, and intent. It reads between the lines. It knows when to push and when to back off.

That's what makes Buddy feel different to anything you've tried before. The responses aren't scripted. The emotional range is real.

What "Agentic AI" actually means

Most AI talks.

GoMaiT's Agentic tier actually DOES things.

That email you've been avoiding for two weeks? Buddy drafts it in your voice, you read it over, you say send, and it sends it for you. Your CV? Talk through your experience for five minutes and Buddy builds the document, formats it properly, and emails it directly to whoever you tell it to. Calendar a mess? Buddy looks at it, reorganises it, and makes the changes. Expense receipts in a pile? It logs them, summarises them, and produces the report. Every single action is shown to you before anything happens. You approve, then it acts. Nothing goes anywhere without your say-so. This is a new kind of help, and it only exists because Claude is capable enough to pull it off.

This technology is new. A year ago it didn't exist at this level. GoMaiT is built on the cutting edge of it. Not to show off, but because this is the first time AI has been capable enough to actually help a brain like yours in a way that feels real.

Built for where you actually are

Every feature works inside the chaos. Not after you've cleaned it up.

Buddy

Not a chatbot. Not a coach. The first thing you do in GoMaiT is give Buddy a name. From that moment, every response and check-in uses it. Not "Hello." Hey Mike. That's what makes it feel like yours.

You talk to Buddy by typing or voice. It talks back in whichever of the three modes you've chosen, and the same words land completely differently depending on what you need that day.

Buddy also watches quietly in the background. Stressometer creeping up all week? Cavalry hit three times in two days? It checks in softly before you've clocked it yourself. It's not a notification. It's just Buddy paying attention.

Same Situation. Different Mode.

"I still haven't started that report. It's due tomorrow."

Drill Sergeant

"Right, Mike. Stop circling it. Open the document. Write one sentence. That's the only job right now. Go."

Nanny

"That sounds heavy, Mike. Let's not think about the whole thing. Just open it and write one line, even a heading. I'm right here."

Agentic tier

"Already on it, Mike. I've drafted an outline based on your notes and pulled the key points in. Take a look and tell me what to change."
Buddy mode
Drill Sergeant
Nanny
Agentic ✦

✦ Document access and drafting available on Agentic tier. Switch modes any time. Buddy adapts instantly.

Cavalry

One tap from anywhere. When everything lands at once, Cavalry is there. Triage the pile, find your footing, or just breathe first. Buddy asks which one you need. No judgment on which you pick.

Elephant Eater

The task that's been sitting there for three weeks. Dump it in. Buddy asks a few questions. Then the Procrastinator Machine kicks in.

Picture a cartoon contraption: gears grinding, steam venting, little gremlins pulling levers. What comes out the other end is the one tiny first step you can actually do right now. Not a plan. Not a system. One step. That's the whole trick.

And when you do it? GoMaiT notices. Buddy celebrates in a way that feels earned, not hollow.

The Task Going In

"Sort out my tax return and expenses from the last six months"

↓

One First Step

"Open your bank app and screenshot January. Just January. Nothing else."

Also Inside GoMaiT

Focus Mode Pattern Break Therapy Lounge Break Glass Time Machine Trophy System End of Day Ritual Burnout Watch Medication Reminder

Trophies are earned through real progress, not streaks. Rookie, Contender, Fighter, Warrior, Champion, Veteran, Legend. You work your way up by actually doing things, not just showing up.

Burnout Watch runs quietly in the background. Buddy notices when your patterns shift. Stressometer creeping up, Cavalry used more often, interaction changing. It checks in softly before you've registered something's wrong.

Medication Reminder is free on every tier. Always. It will never sit behind a paywall.

And yes, there's a whole fun layer. This was non-negotiable from day one.

The founder's take: if it isn't at least a bit fun, it won't get used. Full stop. So GoMaiT is loaded with it. Clear a task you've been avoiding for three weeks and a hallelujah choir goes off. Finish a Focus Mode session and Buddy celebrates like you just ran a marathon. The Procrastinator Machine has actual cartoon gremlin energy: steam, gears, chaos, then one perfect answer out the other end. There are memes. There are sound effects. There are moments where the app takes the absolute mickey out of the situation you're in, and you laugh, and somehow that's the thing that breaks the paralysis.

This is a deliberate design philosophy, not decoration. A brain that struggles to start tasks doesn't need another earnest wellness app adding to the pressure. It needs something that understands the absurdity of the whole situation. Knowing what to do and not being able to do it is objectively ridiculous when you step back, and it meets you there with a bit of humanity and a laugh before helping you move. Fun is the delivery mechanism. It always was.

0

streak mechanics. Ever.
A missed day is a missed day. Not a failure.

Under the bonnet

Built to the edge of what's possible.

Most apps in this space are reminders with a colour theme. GoMaiT is built differently. Here's what's actually running underneath.

🧠

Buddy never forgets.

Every conversation is stored as a memory embedding. Not just a log. A growing map of you that Buddy builds over time, searchable and specific. Come back after six months and Buddy still knows you. Your patterns. Your struggles. What worked. What didn't. Most apps forget you the moment you close them. Buddy doesn't.

📳

You'll feel the difference.

Every feature in GoMaiT has its own haptic signature. The Cavalry press feels different to clearing a task. The Stressometer dropping has its own quiet pulse. Your phone is a sensory device and GoMaiT uses it properly. Haptics are design material here, not decoration. For a brain like yours, what you feel matters as much as what you see.

☕

The body double in your pocket.

Working alongside another person (the coffee shop effect) is one of the most consistently documented strategies for ADHD focus. Buddy's ambient presence during Focus Mode is that body double, personalised and always available. You don't need to schedule it, ask for it or hope someone's available. The science is solid and GoMaiT is the first app that actually builds it in properly.

🎙️

Talk to Buddy. Actually talk.

Voice isn't a future feature bolted on later. It's built in from day one, on-device, completely private, zero network cost, works offline. Log a task by voice. Brain dump without typing. Tell Buddy how you're feeling when you can't find the words to write it. For a brain that runs faster than your fingers, being able to just talk changes how the whole thing feels.

🎭

A companion that actually adapts.

Three modes, and the difference is real. The way Buddy speaks, the pressure it applies, the warmth it gives, all shift based on where you are today. Need pushing? Drill Sergeant shows up: direct, short, activating. Having a hard day? Nanny mode: barely a whisper, just present. This is emotional intelligence built into the architecture, not a setting hidden in a menu.

👁️

Watching before you hit the wall.

Burnout Watch runs in the background without you touching a thing. Buddy tracks your mode selections, Stressometer trajectory and usage patterns, then quietly checks in when the data suggests you're heading somewhere bad. It doesn't ask how you feel. It watches. It catches it early, before you've even named it. An app that sees the burnout arriving before you do is something most people with ADHD have never had.

Built different

Fun is the whole point.

Every other app in this space takes itself very seriously. GoMaiT doesn't. That wasn't an accident. It's the founding philosophy.

The Procrastinator Machine

Dump in the impossible task. Out comes one perfectly sized first step. Every time.

Meet George G. Gerbil.

Time Traveller Extraordinaire. Lives in the Time Machine feature. Has impeccable manners. Never sits still for long. G stands for Great — but don't tell him you know that.

The founder's view is simple: if using an app feels like work, someone with ADD or ADHD will stop using it. The fun layer isn't decoration. It's what gets you back in on the hard days.

🎵

Sound effects that land

Clear a task you've been avoiding for three weeks and a hallelujah choir goes off. Not a ping. A choir. Because that actually deserved celebrating.

🎪

The Procrastinator Machine

Steam, gears, chaos. You dump in the impossible task. Out comes one perfectly sized first step. The Crastlins figured it out.

😂

Memes that get it

GoMaiT knows the memes because the founder lives the memes. There are moments where the app takes the absolute mickey out of the situation you're in. Somehow that's what breaks it.

🏆

Wins that feel real

Buddy celebrates properly when you do something hard. Not hollow encouragement. Recognition that lands because it comes from something that actually understands what that took.

A brain that struggles to start doesn't need more pressure. It needs something that sees the absurdity in the whole situation, meets you there, has a laugh with you, then helps you move. That's GoMaiT.

Agentic tier

Buddy doesn't just talk to you.
On Agentic, it does things for you.

The admin pile that never moves. The emails sitting unanswered for weeks. The CV that needs writing. GoMaiT's agentic layer handles the drafting, organising and preparing. The life admin that drains you most, so you've got more left in the tank for the things only you can do. It shows you everything first. Nothing goes anywhere without you saying so.

✉️

Email Agent

Drafts replies in your voice. You review, edit, and confirm. Never sends without you.

📅

Calendar Agent

Organises your schedule, spots conflicts, suggests time for things you keep pushing.

📄

Document Agent

Letters, reports, applications. Tell Buddy what you need and it builds the first draft.

🎙️

Voice CV Builder

Talk through your experience. Buddy turns it into a professional CV. No blank page, no freeze.

💷

Accounts & Expenses

Receipts, summaries, expense logs. The financial admin you avoid. Handled.

🌅

Morning Briefing

What matters today, what can wait, and one thing to start with. Before the overwhelm hits.

🎙️

Voice note · 1 min 52 sec

"Build me a CV from my voice notes. I've been in project management for eight years and I want something that actually shows my progression properly."

Join waitlist

Your data

What you tell Buddy stays with Buddy.

You're sharing your worst days, your fears, your mental health, your voice. That deserves a straight answer about what happens to it.

🔒

We don't sell your data. Full stop.

Not now, not if the business grows, not ever. Your information is not the product. GoMaiT makes money through subscriptions. That's the whole model. Your data has no part in it.

🎙️

Your voice never leaves your phone.

Voice input is processed on-device. Completely private, no network cost, works offline. When you talk to Buddy, the audio stays on your phone. It doesn't go to a server. It doesn't go anywhere.

💙

Mental health data gets extra care.

The crisis layer, the Therapy Lounge, the hard conversations — all of it is treated as what it is: sensitive health data. It's not scraped. It's not analysed for ads. It's not used for anything other than helping you. That's it.

✅

On the Agentic tier, you see everything first.

Buddy drafts. You approve. Nothing sends, nothing books, nothing files without you saying so. Every single action is shown to you before it happens. You pull the trigger. Always.

The short version: what happens in GoMaiT stays in GoMaiT. Full privacy policy live before launch.

Register My Interest

Questions

The things you're probably thinking right now

Honest answers. No marketing fluff.

I've tried apps before. They never work for me. Why would this be different?
Most apps assume you'll show up consistently, maintain a system, and not miss a day. That's the whole problem right there. GoMaiT was built by someone with ADD, for his family and for people like them. It works in the mess. It doesn't need you to get organised first. Miss a week. Buddy doesn't mention it. There's no system to fall off.
Is this just another productivity app I'll use for a week and forget?
That depends on whether you use it. What we can tell you is that we've deliberately removed everything that makes apps fail for this kind of brain: streak counters, complicated setup, guilt when you go quiet. Come back after a month and Buddy picks up without a word about where you've been. The rest is up to you.
Is it only for people with ADHD or ADD?
No, and the distinction matters. ADD and ADHD are related but different, and GoMaiT isn't built exclusively for either. The founder has ADD, not ADHD. No formal diagnosis, but he's lived every symptom his whole life and has no doubt about it whatsoever. The feeling the app is built around, knowing what you need to do and not being able to start, shows up across ADD, ADHD, autism, anxiety, depression, and plenty of people who've never had any diagnosis at all. We don't ask for a label. If the feeling resonates, the app is for you.
What does "agentic" actually mean?
It means Buddy can actually do things, not just talk about them. Draft that email you've been avoiding. Build a document. Organise your calendar. Turn a voice note into a CV. You see everything before anything goes anywhere. Nothing sends without you saying so.
Is my data private? Especially my mental health data?
We don't sell it. We don't advertise with it. What you share with Buddy stays with Buddy. The mental health and crisis layer gets extra care. It's there to help you, full stop. Full privacy policy at launch. Short version: what happens in GoMaiT stays in GoMaiT.
What if I miss days? Does GoMaiT judge me for it?
No streak counters. No broken chains. Come back after two weeks and Buddy says hello like you never left, no nudges, no passive-aggressive "welcome back after 14 days". A missed day is just a day. What you do today is what matters.
When does it launch and what platforms?
iOS and Android, both at the same time. Waitlist members get in first and get the early bird pricing. Signing up is free and doesn't commit you to anything. It just puts you at the front of the queue.
What does the lifetime deal actually include?
50 agentic actions a month, every month, for life, at a single one-off payment. It covers the Agentic tier as it exists at launch, plus future feature additions to that tier. It doesn't include any new tiers created above Agentic, changes to the 50 actions cap, or premium add-ons introduced after purchase. No money changes hands until the app is ready and the deal formally opens. If it doesn't ship, you owe nothing.

Wear the feeling.

Print-on-demand merch launching with the app. No inventory, no waste. Lines that mean something to anyone who has been there.

Don't Feed the Wolves You Are Not Alone Spectrum Dwellers On Your Feet Recruit Feed the Lambs not the Wolves
Join to be notified

"You are simultaneously the founder, the product manager, the target user, the test subject, and the primary case study. You are building the Cavalry button while desperately needing someone to hit the Cavalry button for you. You are designing the Elephant Eater while staring at your own elephant."

I only worked it out a few years ago. Why I'd struggled my whole life. When it clicked, the thing I felt most was relief. Not failure. Relief. Suddenly there was a reason. I wasn't lazy. I wasn't broken. I wasn't the kid who couldn't keep up. My brain just works differently, and once I understood that, I stopped fighting myself.

I opened my first business at ten years old. School made me feel like I had nothing to offer. But the moment I did something myself, sold something, earned something off my own back, that changed. "I can actually do this. I have a brain. I did that. Me." That feeling never left. It just took a long time to understand why it had always come so much harder than it should have.

Two of my kids show the same patterns. My youngest has an ADHD diagnosis at 11. My older son has no formal diagnosis but the struggles are the same ones I know well, the paralysis, the difficulty starting, the gap between knowing and doing. I'm building this for them as much as for myself, and for everyone stuck in the Swamp right now, knowing exactly what they need to do and not able to move.

GoMaiT came from all of that. A family that needed it. Years of not understanding why things were harder than they should have been. And finally getting some answers and deciding to do something with them.

MB

Michael Boyle

Founder, GoMaiT  ·  Enniskillen, Northern Ireland  ·  ADD, 52. Self-identified, no doubt about it.

Still here? For someone who can't start things, you just read a lot of words. Maybe we're onto something.

You are here now.
That's enough.

GoMaiT isn't a productivity app or a habit tracker. It's not trying to fix you. It just helps you move, one step at a time, from wherever you are right now.

You're on the list. Buddy will be in touch soon.
